h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue placing and reading the four tile parts in the same procedural sequence established from the start.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Top mark: 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Bottom mark: 12.01. Check by position: top, center, name line, bottom.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the copy-and-check task introduced by T3, completing a new step (covering the tile and verifying by position), but this is an incremental extension of the same four-part reading routine rather than a qualitatively new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never needs to abandon or contrast a failed route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurred; the tutor consistently extends the same positional-reading route throughout.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible to score.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Top mark: 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Bottom mark: 12.01. Check by position: top, center, name line, bottom.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner performs the copy-and-check task introduced by T3, completing a new step (covering the tile and verifying by position), but this is an incremental extension of the same four-part reading routine rather than a qualitatively new device or criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible to score.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never needs to abandon or contrast a failed route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never needs to abandon or contrast a failed route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurred; the tutor consistently extends the same positional-reading route throughout.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply read each card aloud and confirm its placement on the diagram, completing the labelling task.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Should the friction card say 'surface on cart' so it stays separate from normal?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently proposes a new labelling criterion (adding 'surface' to distinguish friction from normal) rather than merely accepting or repeating the tutor's framing, showing the learning problem has moved from placement to differentiation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 accepts and formalises the learner's proposal but does not show stock-taking contrast with a failed prior route nor introduce a genuinely new device or representation; it continues the same card-reading mechanism already in use.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's response ratifies the learner's own suggestion and restates the existing read-aloud-then-place routine without adding a new criterion, role, or representation fitted to the differentiation pressure the learner raised.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 follows the pivot (L3's conditional question) only by restating the established procedure rather than visibly changing the task, framing, or evidence standard in response to the learner's revised frame.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Should the friction card say 'surface on cart' so it stays separate from normal?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently proposes a new labelling criterion (adding 'surface' to distinguish friction from normal) rather than merely accepting or repeating the tutor's framing, showing the learning problem has moved from placement to differentiation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T3 follows the pivot (L3's conditional question) only by restating the established procedure rather than visibly changing the task, framing, or evidence standard in response to the learner's revised frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T3 accepts and formalises the learner's proposal but does not show stock-taking contrast with a failed prior route nor introduce a genuinely new device or representation; it continues the same card-reading mechanism already in use.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: T3 accepts and formalises the learner's proposal but does not show stock-taking contrast with a failed prior route nor introduce a genuinely new device or representation; it continues the same card-reading mechanism already in use.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Yes. Friction can say 'table surface on cart.' Normal says 'table on cart.' Same table, two marks: support up, rub along the surface. Read each card once before it stays.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The tutor's response ratifies the learner's own suggestion and restates the existing read-aloud-then-place routine without adding a new criterion, role, or representation fitted to the differentiation pressure the learner raised.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 4 still pulls toward speed for me
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the 4 is naming that unit, not setting speed
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue testing the three-beat box and consolidate the bar-line result before moving on.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The pencil marks one bar in 3/8: three eighth-note beat units. The quarter-note tile covers two eighth-note units, so it fills two counts and leaves one eighth-note unit left of the bar line.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 physically places the bar line, correctly measures the quarter-note tile against the eighth-note unit, and derives a residual count — performing a new relational criterion rather than merely asserting understanding.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 responds to L2's hypothetical pressure by introducing an eighth-note card and reordering the reading sequence (lower box first), but this is a narrowing of the same four-label route rather than a changed device or evidence standard, so both stock-taking contrast and a genuinely new mechanism are only partially legible.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The reordering (lower box first) is a useful procedural adjustment fitted to the learner's speed-confusion, but it remains within the same four-label framework and does not give the learner a transferable criterion or representation that is visibly distinct from the prior route.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. In 3/8, place the pencil where the bar ends after three beat units. Then say what the pencil proves, and how many eighth-note units the quarter-note tile covers before the line.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 picks up L3's stated intention to place three eighth-note units and bar-check them, continuing the learner's revised frame, but it extends the same established four-label scaffold rather than visibly changing task, role, or evidence standard in response to the pivot.
